36→package com.ichi2.utils
37→
38→import android.net.Uri
39→import android.webkit.MimeTypeMap
40→import java.io.File
41→import java.util.Locale
42→
43→/** Clone of RestrictedApi functionality  */
44→object AssetHelper {
45→    /**
46→     Returns the extension of [path].
47→     It uses [MimeTypeMap.getFileExtensionFromUrl], with the path transformed into a Uri.
48→     */
49→    fun getFileExtensionFromFilePath(path: String): String {
50→        return MimeTypeMap.getFileExtensionFromUrl(
51→            Uri.fromFile(File(path)).toString().lowercase(
52→                Locale.ROOT
53→            )
54→        )
55→    }
56→
57→    /**
58→     * Use [MimeTypeMap.getMimeTypeFromExtension] to guess MIME type or return the
59→     * "text/plain" if it can't guess.
60→     *
61→     * Copy of [androidx.webkit.internal.AssetHelper.guessMimeType]
62→     *
63→     * @param path path of the file to guess its MIME type.
64→     * @return MIME type guessed from file extension or "text/plain".
65→     */
66→    fun guessMimeType(path: String): String {
67→        return when (val extension = getFileExtensionFromFilePath(path)) {
68→            "js" -> "text/javascript"
69→            "mjs" -> "text/javascript"
70→            "json" -> "application/json"
71→            else -> MimeTypeMap.getSingleton().getMimeTypeFromExtension(extension) ?: "text/plain"
72→        }
73→    }
74→}

 21→import android.webkit.WebResourceResponse
 22→import android.webkit.WebView
 23→import android.webkit.WebViewClient
 24→import androidx.core.view.isVisible
 25→import com.google.android.material.color.MaterialColors
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.OnPageFinishedCallback
 27→import com.ichi2.utils.AssetHelper
 28→import com.ichi2.utils.toRGBHex
 29→import timber.log.Timber
 30→import java.io.IOException
 31→
 32→/**
 33→ * Base WebViewClient to be used on [PageFragment]
 34→ */
 35→open class PageWebViewClient : WebViewClient() {
 36→
 37→    /** Wait for the provided promise to complete before showing the WebView */
 38→    open val promiseToWaitFor: String? = null
 39→
 40→    var onPageFinishedCallback: OnPageFinishedCallback? = null
 41→
 42→    override fun shouldInterceptRequest(
 43→        view: WebView,
 44→        request: WebResourceRequest
 45→    ): WebResourceResponse? {
 46→        val path = request.url.path
 47→        if (request.method == "GET" && path?.startsWith("/backend/web") == true) {
 48→            try {
 49→                val mime = AssetHelper.guessMimeType(path)
 50→                val inputStream = view.context.assets.open(path.substring(1))
 51→                return WebResourceResponse(mime, null, inputStream)
 52→            } catch (_: IOException) {
 53→                Timber.w("%s not found", path)
 54→            }
 55→        }
 56→        return super.shouldInterceptRequest(view, request)
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    override fun onPageStarted(view: WebView?, url: String?, favicon: Bitmap?) {
 60→        super.onPageStarted(view, url, favicon)
 61→        view?.let { webView ->
 62→            val bgColor = MaterialColors.getColor(webView, android.R.attr.colorBackground).toRGBHex()
 63→            webView.evaluateAfterDOMContentLoaded(
 64→                """document.body.style.setProperty("background-color", "$bgColor", "important");
 65→                    console.log("Background color set");"""
 66→            )
 67→        }
 68→    }
 69→
 70→    override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
 71→        super.onPageFinished(view, url)
 72→        if (view == null) return
 73→        onPageFinishedCallback?.onPageFinished(view)
 74→        if (promiseToWaitFor == null) {
 75→            /** [PageFragment.webView] is invisible by default to avoid flashes while
 76→             * the page is loaded, and can be made visible again after it finishes loading */
 77→            Timber.v("displaying WebView")
 78→            view.isVisible = true
 79→        } else {
 80→            view.evaluateJavascript("""$promiseToWaitFor.then(() => { console.log("page-fully-loaded:"); window.location.href = "page-fully-loaded:" } )""") {
 81→                Timber.v("waiting for '$promiseToWaitFor' before displaying WebView")
 82→            }
 83→        }
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    override fun shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view: WebView?, request: WebResourceRequest?): Boolean {
 87→        if (view == null || request == null) return super.shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view, request)
 88→        if (request.url.toString() == "page-fully-loaded:") {
 89→            Timber.v("displaying WebView after '$promiseToWaitFor' executed")
 90→            view.isVisible = true
 91→            return true
 92→        }
 93→        return super.shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view, request)
 94→    }

174→                paramView: View,

187→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
188→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_SHOW_TRANSIENT_BARS_BY_SWIPE
189→                    hide(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())

191→            }

195→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).removeView(customView)
196→                // show system bars back
197→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
198→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_DEFAULT
199→                    show(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())

27→/**
28→ * Headers that allow JavaScript to access content loaded in `<object>` or `<iframe>` tags.
29→ * This is necessary for templates that load SVG files and manipulate them via JavaScript.
30→ * See: https://github.com/nickersk/Anki-Android/issues/16067
31→ */
32→private val CORS_HEADERS: Map<String, String> = mapOf(
33→    "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" to "*"
34→)
35→
36→fun Context.getViewerAssetLoader(domain: String): WebViewAssetLoader {
37→    val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(this)
38→    return WebViewAssetLoader.Builder()
39→        .setHttpAllowed(true)
40→        .setDomain(domain)
41→        .addPathHandler("/") { path: String ->
42→            if (path == "favicon.ico") {
43→                return@addPathHandler WebResourceResponse(null, null, ByteArrayInputStream(ByteArray(0)))
44→            }
45→            try {
46→                val file = File(mediaDir, path)
47→                val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
48→                WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), "UTF-8", 200, "OK", CORS_HEADERS, inputStream)
49→            } catch (e: Exception) {
50→                Timber.d("File not found: %s", path)
51→                null
52→            }
53→        }
54→        .build()
55→}

The fix is complete. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Summary

**Bug:** SVG CSS updates via JavaScript were not rendering correctly in AnkiDroid. When a card template loaded an SVG file using an `<object>` tag and tried to modify its styles via JavaScript (e.g., `img_obj.contentDocument.querySelectorAll(...).forEach(note => note.style.fill = "...")`), the changes weren't applied because WebView blocked cross-document access.

**Root Cause:** The `WebResourceResponse` returned by `ViewerAssetLoader` didn't include CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) headers. When the main HTML is loaded via `loadDataWithBaseURL` and then an `<object>` tag loads an SVG, the browser/WebView treats this as a cross-origin request, blocking JavaScript access to `contentDocument` without proper CORS headers.

**Fix:** Updated `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerAssetLoader.kt` to include `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` header in the response for all media files. This allows JavaScript running in card templates to access the content document of `<object>` and `<iframe>` elements.

The change uses the full `WebResourceResponse` constructor that accepts response headers:
```kotlin
WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), "UTF-8", 200, "OK", CORS_HEADERS, inputStream)
```

This fix affects both:
1. `AbstractFlashcardViewer` - the main card review screen
2. `CardViewerFragment` - the card preview screen

Both use `getViewerAssetLoader()` to serve media files, so the CORS headers will now be included for all media responses, enabling the SVG manipulation scenario described in the bug report.
